mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, which would lead to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, which would lead to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months indicates rising economic activity, which would lead to higher demand for raw materials and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Personal Consumption Expenditures (RSAFS) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the same period, signals a strong consumer sector and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 1% in 3 months and UNRATE falls > 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strong-consumer periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate over the past 3 months, combined with a 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the same period, signals a tightening monetary cycle and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 1% in 3 months and CPIAUCSL rises > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 6 months, combined with a 2% increase in the Average Hourly Earnings (not available in the catalog), signals a tightening labor market and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the New Privately Owned Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 6 months signals a strong housing market and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in strong-housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
